# deploy

<!-- archie:ai-start -->

> Kubernetes deployment artefacts for OpenMeter: two independently versioned, self-contained Helm charts (openmeter, benthos-collector) published to the GHCR OCI registry, plus a local kind cluster config. deploy/charts owns only the shared helm-docs generation pipeline consumed by both charts; no shared manifests, CRDs, or cluster-scoped RBAC live at this level.

## Patterns

**helm-docs via shared template** — Both chart READMEs are generated by helm-docs from deploy/charts/template.md plus each chart's values.yaml comments; READMEs are never hand-edited. (`make -C deploy/charts docs  # regenerates both chart READMEs`)
**OCI Helm registry distribution** — Charts are packaged and pushed to ghcr.io OCI registry on release tags rather than a traditional index-based Helm repo. (`helm package deploy/charts/openmeter --version <VERSION> --destination build/helm`)
**Enabled-guard + configmap override for optional infrastructure** — Optional features (bundled Kafka, ClickHouse, Redis) use an enabled: bool guard in resource templates AND a matching override block in configmap.yaml so disabled services inject no config. (`{{- if .Values.kafka.enabled }}...{{- end }} paired with the kafka section in configmap.yaml`)
**Self-contained child charts** — Each chart bundles everything it needs; all OpenMeter binaries share one image and tag in the openmeter chart, aligned to Chart.yaml appVersion. (`deploy/charts/openmeter/values.yaml image tag aligned with Chart.yaml appVersion.`)

## Key Files

| File | Role | Watch For |
|------|------|-----------|
| `deploy/charts/Makefile` | Drives helm-docs README generation for both charts. | Adding a third chart requires a new README.tmpl.md and an update to this docs target. |
| `deploy/charts/template.md` | Shared helm-docs template consumed by both child charts. | Changes affect both READMEs; keep it generic with no chart-specific content. |
| `deploy/charts/openmeter/templates/configmap.yaml` | Renders OpenMeter config.yaml from Helm values; all optional infrastructure blocks need enabled guards. | A missing enabled guard injects config for disabled services. |
| `deploy/kind.yaml` | Local kind cluster config with ingress-ready node label and 80/443 port mappings. | Port mappings must match the chart service/ingress defaults to avoid local routing mismatches. |

## Anti-Patterns

- Hand-editing a child chart README.md — it is generated by helm-docs and edits are overwritten on next `make docs`.
- Adding a third chart without a corresponding README.tmpl.md and Makefile docs update.
- Storing shared Kubernetes manifests (CRDs, cluster-scoped RBAC) at deploy/charts level — each chart must be fully self-contained.
- Publishing charts without aligning Chart.yaml appVersion with the container image tag in values.yaml.
- Adding optional infrastructure without both an enabled guard in the template AND a matching override block in configmap.yaml.

## Decisions

- **Shared template.md at deploy/charts level rather than duplicated per chart.** — Avoids README format drift between the two charts; single edit point for documentation structure.
- **OCI registry (ghcr.io) as the distribution channel, not a traditional Helm repository index.** — OCI registries provide content-addressable artefact storage and align with the container image distribution model already used for application images.
- **All OpenMeter binaries share one image and tag in the openmeter chart.** — The multi-stage Dockerfile produces all binaries from one image, so a single tag keeps deployed components version-consistent.

## Example: Regenerate chart docs and package a chart for OCI release

```
make -C deploy/charts docs
helm package deploy/charts/openmeter --version $VERSION --destination build/helm
helm push build/helm/openmeter-$VERSION.tgz oci://ghcr.io/openmeterio/helm-charts
```

<!-- archie:ai-end -->
